prompt.en: Which of the following correctly classifies four problems by two-pointer sub-pattern? (slow/fast = both pointers start at the left and advance at different rates; convergent = one pointer starts at each end and they move toward each other)
prompt.es: ¿Cuál de las siguientes opciones clasifica correctamente cuatro problemas según el subpatrón de two-pointer? (slow/fast = ambos punteros comienzan a la izquierda y avanzan a distinto ritmo; convergente = un puntero en cada extremo, se acercan entre sí)

label.en: move-zeros → slow/fast; remove-val-in-place → slow/fast; two-sum-sorted → convergent; palindrome-check → convergent
label.es: move-zeros → slow/fast; remove-val-in-place → slow/fast; two-sum-sorted → convergent; palindrome-check → convergent

label.en: move-zeros → convergent; remove-val-in-place → convergent; two-sum-sorted → slow/fast; palindrome-check → slow/fast
label.es: move-zeros → convergent; remove-val-in-place → convergent; two-sum-sorted → slow/fast; palindrome-check → slow/fast

label.en: move-zeros → slow/fast; remove-val-in-place → convergent; two-sum-sorted → convergent; palindrome-check → slow/fast
label.es: move-zeros → slow/fast; remove-val-in-place → convergent; two-sum-sorted → convergent; palindrome-check → slow/fast

label.en: All four use the convergent sub-pattern because all require scanning from both directions
label.es: Los cuatro usan el subpatrón convergente porque todos requieren escanear desde ambas direcciones

explanation.en: move-zeros and remove-val-in-place both use slow/fast: a slow pointer tracks the write position and a fast pointer scans ahead, copying valid elements forward — both pointers start at index 0. two-sum-sorted and palindrome-check both use convergent: left starts at index 0, right at n-1, and they meet in the middle. Option C is a common mix-up — remove-val-in-place is not convergent; you never need to scan from the right because all valid elements are simply written to the front.
explanation.es: move-zeros y remove-val-in-place usan slow/fast: el puntero lento rastrea la posición de escritura y el rápido escanea hacia adelante copiando elementos válidos — ambos comienzan en el índice 0. two-sum-sorted y palindrome-check usan convergente: left comienza en el índice 0, right en n-1, y se encuentran en el centro. La opción C es una confusión común — remove-val-in-place no es convergente; nunca necesitas escanear desde la derecha porque todos los elementos válidos simplemente se escriben al frente.
